title: GLYCEROL
description: Glycerol is a three-carbon sugar alcohol that acts as an osmotic agent to enhance fluid retention in the body. When consumed with adequate fluids, it creates a hyperhydrated state by drawing and retaining water in the intravascular and intracellular spaces, which can improve endurance performance, thermoregulation, and muscle pumps during exercise.

research.title: Guidelines for Glycerol Use in Hyperhydration and Rehydration Associated with Exercise
research.summary: Endurance athletes intending to hyperhydrate with glycerol should ingest 1.2 g/kg bodyweight in 26 mL/kg bodyweight of fluid over 60 minutes, 30 minutes prior to exercise. Glycerol ingestion before, during, or following exercise is likely to improve hydration state and performance.

research.title: Glycerol: Biochemistry, Pharmacokinetics and Clinical and Practical Applications
research.summary: Comprehensive review of glycerol's mechanism as a hyperhydrating agent through osmotic gradient creation in circulation, examining its pharmacokinetics and applications in athletic performance and medical settings.

research.title: The Effect of Glycerol Supplements on Aerobic and Anaerobic Performance of Athletes and Sedentary Subjects
research.summary: Study comparing glycerol supplementation effects on both trained athletes and sedentary individuals, finding improvements in both aerobic endurance capacity and anaerobic performance measures when properly dosed.

research.title: Glycerol Use in Hyperhydration and Rehydration: Scientific Update
research.summary: Updated scientific review examining the efficacy of glycerol for both pre-exercise hyperhydration protocols and post-exercise rehydration strategies, confirming benefits for maintaining plasma volume and delaying dehydration.

sentiment.summary: Users consistently report significant improvements in muscle pumps, vascularity, and workout performance with glycerol supplementation. Most users recommend it as a core non-stimulant pre-workout ingredient, though some note difficulty with sourcing quality products and occasional gastrointestinal discomfort.

effects.label: Dose-Dependent Response:
effects.description: Effectiveness strongly correlates with proper dosing protocol (1.0-1.2g/kg bodyweight) combined with adequate fluid intake

effects.label: Form Matters:
effects.description: Glycerol monostearate (65% glycerol) and pure glycerol powder show better results than lower-concentration forms

effects.label: Timing Critical:
effects.description: Most effective when consumed 30-60 minutes pre-workout with substantial water intake (20-26 mL/kg bodyweight)

effects.label: Individual Variation:
effects.description: Response varies significantly based on hydration status, exercise intensity, and environmental conditions (heat, humidity)

dosage.label: Pre-Workout Hyperhydration:
dosage.description: 1.0-1.2g glycerol per kg bodyweight consumed with 20-26 mL fluid per kg bodyweight, 30-60 minutes before exercise

dosage.label: During Exercise:
dosage.description: 0.125g/kg bodyweight in 5 mL/kg fluid volume to delay dehydration during prolonged sessions

dosage.label: Post-Exercise Rehydration:
dosage.description: 1.0g/kg bodyweight added to each 1.5L of rehydration fluid to accelerate plasma volume restoration

dosage.label: Practical Dosing:
dosage.description: For average 80kg individual, approximately 80-100g glycerol powder mixed with 1.5-2L water pre-workout

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Distress:
sideEffects.description: Some users report nausea, bloating, or stomach discomfort, particularly with higher doses or insufficient acclimation period

sideEffects.label: Light-Headedness:
sideEffects.description: Occasional reports of dizziness or feeling off-balance, especially when not consumed with adequate fluids

sideEffects.label: Frequent Urination:
sideEffects.description: While glycerol promotes fluid retention, some users still experience increased urination, particularly during initial adaptation

sideEffects.label: Headaches:
sideEffects.description: Rare reports of headaches, possibly related to rapid fluid shifts or electrolyte imbalances when not properly hydrated

availability.label: Specialized Sourcing:
availability.description: Available primarily through sports nutrition retailers and bulk supplement suppliers; less common than other pre-workout ingredients

availability.label: Form Variations:
availability.description: Available as glycerol monostearate powder (65% glycerol), pure glycerol powder, or in proprietary pre-workout formulations

availability.label: Quality Concerns:
availability.description: Users emphasize importance of purchasing from reputable brands due to variability in product purity and concentration
